title: Isolation of exosomal RNA from serum or plasma using ultracentrifugation and the Qiagen miRNeasy Micro kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ahghy","text":"This protocol describes how to isolate exosomal RNA from serum/plasma by using ultracentrifugation for exosomal enrichment followed by the Qiagen miRNeasy Micro kit for RNA isolation. It supercedes an earlier protocol (http://dx.doi.org/10.1038/protex.2015.111). The new protocol is based on our experience vetting the original protocol in multiple labs.  The major change is a decrease in input volume of biofluid from 1 mL to 500 µL in order to match the input volume of other protocols the ERC consortium is developing in tandem.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"upyi0","text":"Extracellular RNAs (exRNAs) have been found to play an important role in intercellular communication in the body. exRNAs are present in biofluids, in complexes with lipoproteins and ribonucleoproteins, and in extracellular vesicles such as exosomes and microvesicles. Further study and characterization of exRNAs and their carriers could lead to identification of new biomarkers and have potential for development of novel therapeutics.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Start with 500 µL serum or plasma.
Bring up volume to fill ultracentrifuge tube (2.3-2.5 mL) with PBS.
Centrifuge for 70 min at 100,000 x g at 4°C.
Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in PBS to fill ultracentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge for 70 min at 100,000 x g at 4°C.
Resuspend pellet in 50 µL PBS and store at -80°C, or
Proceed to miRNeasy RNA isolation by adding 700 µL Qiazol to pellet.
The authors declare no competing financial interests.
